result:
Done — the spec is fully implemented and the build passes cleanly with no warnings (1 of 10 build cycles used).

I added the three missing static methods to `Spans.cs`, matching the existing loop-based style of `TotalCovered`:

- **`ContainsPoint`** — linear scan checking `starts[i] <= x < ends[i]`, respecting the half-open semantics.
- **`ClipCovered`** — for each interval, intersects it with the `[lo, hi)` window via `Math.Max`/`Math.Min` and adds the overlap length only when positive, so partial overlaps contribute their overlapping part and disjoint intervals contribute nothing.
- **`GapCount`** — counts consecutive pairs where `starts[i+1] - ends[i] > 0`, so touching intervals form no gap and zero or one interval yields 0.

`TotalCovered` is untouched, and everything is pure integer computation with no I/O or global state.
